title: ConvertKit vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool is best for your needs.
h1: ConvertKit vs Constant Contact: Detailed Feature & Price Comparison
intro: ConvertKit and Constant Contact are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. ConvertKit is purpose-built for creators, bloggers, and course sellers, while Constant Contact focuses on nonprofits and local businesses. Choosing between them depends on your business model, budget, and specific feature requirements.

comparison_table.tool_b: SMS, social media ads, event management

choose_a_if: You're a creator, blogger, or course seller needing advanced automation and landing pages
You want a free tier to test the platform with up to 1,000 subscribers
You need built-in e-commerce functionality integrated with email marketing
choose_b_if: You're looking for the lowest starting price with SMS and social media ad integration
You need event management features for nonprofits or local business promotions
You prefer a platform focused on simplicity and straightforward email campaigns

faq.q: Is ConvertKit better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. ConvertKit scores higher overall (4.4 vs 4.0 on G2) and is purpose-built for creators with superior automation and landing pages. However, Constant Contact may be better if you need SMS, social media ads, or event management features at a lower entry price.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Constant Contact starts at $12/month, making it $17 cheaper than ConvertKit's $29/month entry point. However, ConvertKit offers a free tier for up to 1,000 subscribers, while Constant Contact has no free option, making ConvertKit more cost-effective for those starting out.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support subscriber list imports, though you'll need to export your contacts from Constant Contact first. ConvertKit's migration process is straightforward, but check that your automation and tagging structure translates to ConvertKit's visual automation system before switching.
